stem: A automotive testing study measures the fuel consumption (L/100km) for a sample of 25 vehicles. The sample mean is 37.8 L/100km with a sample standard deviation of 5.4 L/100km. Assuming the data are approximately normally distributed, estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Population mean ≈ 37.8 L/100km. 95% CI = [35.6, 40.0]. Point estimate: 37.8 L/100km.

common_failure_modes: Using z-critical value instead of t-critical for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
